# Talk Page Interpretation

## What
Extend the L2 interpreter to classify talk page discussion types when `talk_page_correlated` events are present in the evidence stream.

## Why
Once L1 produces talk-page-correlated events (L1-01 + L1-02), the L2 model can classify what kind of discussion accompanied each content edit. This gives the fandom historian structured labels like "notability challenge", "sourcing dispute", or "neutrality concern" — with confidence scores — instead of raw talk page text.

## Context
Read first:
- `packages/interpreter/src/index.ts` — full model adapter interface, system prompt, user prompt construction, how events are batched and sent to the model
- `packages/evidence-graph/src/schemas/evidence.ts` — `EvidenceEvent` shape, `ModelInterpretation`
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` lines 13-19, 73-84 — L2 contract and invariants

## Implementation
1. Add a new classification dimension to the interpreter: `discussionType`
2. Extend the system prompt (around line 231 of interpreter/src/index.ts) to include a taxonomy of talk page discussion types:
   - `notability_challenge` — discussion about whether the topic/article meets notability guidelines
   - `sourcing_dispute` — debate about source reliability or citation adequacy
   - `neutrality_concern` — dispute about NPOV, bias, or due weight
   - `content_deletion` — proposal or discussion of removing content
   - `content_addition` — proposal or justification for adding content
   - `naming_dispute` — debate about article/section titles
   - `procedural` — template notices, bot notifications, archival
   - `other` — catch-all
3. When `talk_page_correlated` events appear in the batch, the model should populate `discussionType` in the `ModelInterpretation` alongside `semanticChange`, `confidence`, and `policyDimension`
4. Add `discussionType?: string` to the `ModelInterpretation` interface in evidence-graph if not already present (check first)
5. Each classification carries a confidence score (0.0–1.0)

## Invariants
- **L2 never sees raw talk page text** — it only receives `deterministicFacts` from the L1 talk correlator (edit comment summaries, time deltas)
- Classification is bounded by confidence — low-confidence classifications are flagged as `speculative`
- Model adapter interface must remain pluggable (all five providers must support the new field)

## Acceptance
- [ ] Gate: `bun run build && bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`
- [ ] Write a test in `packages/interpreter/src/__tests__/` that:
  - Passes a batch including `talk_page_correlated` events with deterministic facts
  - Verifies the interpretation output includes `discussionType` with a confidence score
  - Verifies the model is never passed raw wikitext in the prompt
- [ ] Works with at least 2 providers (openai + one other)
- [ ] Update ROADMAP.md status for L2-01 to `done`
